title: TLS Certificate Expiry on Production Load Balancer
symptoms: SSL certificate error on all HTTPS connections to the platform
Browser security warnings blocking user access
Certificate expiry date showing March 23, 2025 in TLS handshake
AWS Certificate Manager showing failed renewal status
DNS validation CNAME record missing from Route 53
root_cause: TLS certificate auto-renewal failed silently because the DNS validation CNAME record was accidentally removed during a DNS zone migration 30 days prior
resolution: Restored the DNS validation CNAME record, triggered manual certificate renewal in ACM, and applied the new certificate to the ALB within 35 minutes
preventive_actions: Implement certificate expiry monitoring with 30-day, 14-day, and 7-day alerts
Add DNS validation record protection with deletion prevention
Create automated certificate renewal verification test
Add certificate expiry check to weekly operational review

timeline.event: All HTTPS connections begin failing with certificate errors
Automated monitoring detects SSL handshake failures
ACM renewal failure identified, DNS validation CNAME missing
DNS validation CNAME record restored in Route 53
Manual certificate renewal triggered in ACM
New certificate issued and applied to ALB

ocr_entities: Reason: DNS validation record not found
embedding_summary: TLS certificate expired on production ALB after auto-renewal silently failed due to missing DNS validation CNAME record removed during DNS zone migration. Complete HTTPS access failure for 35 minutes affecting 52,000 users. Resolved by restoring DNS record and triggering manual certificate renewal.
